prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a krink has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the krink-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a krink, a arn, and a yib. The krink is underneath the arn. The arn is to-the-left-of the yib. The krink has a yellow button on top, a purple button on side, and a green button on bottom. The arn has a purple button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The yib has a green button on top and a yellow button on side. Pressing the yellow button on the krink activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a vask, a lurn, and a jorp. The jorp is underneath the vask. The vask is to-the-left-of the lurn. The vask has a yellow button on top, a yellow button on side, and a purple button on bottom. The lurn has a green button on top and a purple button on bottom. The jorp has a yellow button on top, a purple button on side, and a green button on bottom. People who have interacted with the vask report that its yellow button is the one you press first.

Which object in the perception scenario is the krink-analog, and which button activates it?
